name: Lactic acid
lead: The forgiving AHA. Larger molecule, slower penetration, lower irritation curve. It is also a natural humectant, which is why it leaves skin feeling more hydrated than glycolic. The right exfoliant for sensitive skin and the right starting point for AHA-curious users.

whatItIs.body: actic acid is an alpha-hydroxy acid produced by lactobacilli during sugar fermentation. It is also one of the components your own skin makes as part of natural moisturising factor (NMF), which is why a leave-on lactic serum simultaneously exfoliates and hydrates — a combination glycolic acid cannot match. The molecule is roughly twice the size of glycolic, so it penetrates more slowly and irritates less.

mechanism.b: Loosens corneocyte adhesion at the upper stratum corneum. Slower depth than glycolic.
Acts as a humectant; component of native NMF. Net moisture gain after use.
Mild tyrosinase inhibition at higher concentrations. Adjunctive for PIH and dullness.

evidence.note: 5–12% leave-on improves smoothness and clarity within 8 weeks.
Measurable TEWL reduction. Unique among AHAs.
Slower than glycolic on lines; cleaner tolerance profile.
The headline. Comfortably the most tolerable AHA at equivalent dose.

concentration.b: Nightly is fine. The default for sensitive skin.
Hydration plus exfoliation. The sweet spot for most.
More turnover, still tolerable. Cap here for home use.
In-clinic peels. Not a home product.

pairings.note: Layered humectants. Apply HA first to a damp face, lactic over.
Wait 15–20 min after lactic, then layer for barrier support.
Alternate nights early on. Many tolerate same-night pairing once both are established.
Both acidic; pH stacking is wasteful. AM C, PM lactic.

products.name: Good Genes Lactic Acid
products.note: Premium reference. 5% in a polished vehicle, comfortable nightly.

products.name: Lactic Acid 10% + HA
products.note: Honest dose, basic vehicle. Still the best value entry.

products.name: Lactic Acid 10% Cream
products.note: Indian-market, ceramide base. Good for combination skin.

products.name: Lactic Acid 12% Serum
products.note: Higher dose without losing comfort. Good vehicle work.

faq.q: Lactic or glycolic for first AHA?
faq.a: Lactic. Same exfoliation mechanism, gentler curve, hydration bonus. Move to glycolic only if lactic plateaus and your barrier is comfortable.

faq.q: Why does my skin feel softer, not drier?
faq.a: Lactic is a humectant as well as an exfoliant — one of the few acids that net adds moisture. That's the molecule's signature.

faq.q: Can I use it every night?
faq.a: Most people can. Start three nights a week, build over four weeks. If your retinoid is also nightly, alternate them.
Yes, at standard cosmetic concentrations. One of the few well-studied actives that remains usable through pregnancy.
